**Irshad Kamil** (born 5 September 1971) is an Indian poet and lyricist.[[1]](#cite_note-1) He has works in several Hindi films including *[Once Upon a Time in Mumbaai](/wiki/Once_Upon_a_Time_in_Mumbaai "Once Upon a Time in Mumbaai")*, *[Jab We Met](/wiki/Jab_We_Met "Jab We Met")*, *[Chameli](/wiki/Chameli_(film) "Chameli (film)")*, *[Love Aaj Kal](/wiki/Love_Aaj_Kal_(2009_film) "Love Aaj Kal (2009 film)")*,(2010 Film) Tum Milo Toh Sahi *[Rockstar](/wiki/Rockstar_(2011_film) "Rockstar (2011 film)")*, *[Laila Majnu](/wiki/Laila_Majnu_(2018_film) "Laila Majnu (2018 film)")*, *[Aashiqui 2](/wiki/Aashiqui_2 "Aashiqui 2")*, *[Raanjhanaa](/wiki/Raanjhanaa "Raanjhanaa")*, *[Highway](/wiki/Highway_(2014_Hindi_film) "Highway (2014 Hindi film)")*, *[Tamasha](/wiki/Tamasha_(2015_film) "Tamasha (2015 film)")*, *[Jab Harry Met Sejal](/wiki/Jab_Harry_Met_Sejal "Jab Harry Met Sejal")*, *[Kabir Singh](/wiki/Kabir_Singh "Kabir Singh")*, *[Dunki](/wiki/Dunki_(film) "Dunki (film)")*, and *[Love Aaj Kal](/wiki/Love_Aaj_Kal_(2020_film) "Love Aaj Kal (2020 film)")*.

## Early life and education

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Irshad_Kamil&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Early life and education")]

Kamil was born as the seventh child to his parents in [Malerkotla](/wiki/Malerkotla "Malerkotla"), and belongs to a [Punjabi Muslim](/wiki/Punjabi_Muslim "Punjabi Muslim") family.[[2]](#cite_note-TOI-2)

He studied journalism at the [Panjab University](/wiki/Panjab_University "Panjab University"), followed by postgraduate and PhD degrees in [Hindi](/wiki/Hindi "Hindi").[[2]](#cite_note-TOI-2)
